During my three-week psychiatric nursing rotation in the TERATAI ward at Dr. Sardjito General
Hospital, I experienced a significant shift in my perspective toward individuals with mental health
disorders. Previously, I held negative stereotypes and viewed them as frightening individuals.
However, after interacting directly with them, I realized they were not as intimidating as I had
imagined. This experience opened my eyes to the fact that they are also human beings who require
attention, understanding, and support.
This rotation also played a crucial role in developing my empathy toward patients. I learned to
understand their feelings and needs, and to put myself in their shoes. As a result, I became more
attuned to patients' conditions and able to provide better, more humane nursing care. The empathy
cultivated during this rotation will be an essential asset in my future nursing career.

Very disappointed. Staffs, both nurse and doctors, are very slow, unprofessional, and disrespectful to the patients. Let me explain why (disclaimer: this might not apply to different polyclinics, but hey, let's not discount my experience).

I went to obgyn clinic to get a USG and treatment for my ovarian cyst. I've registered online 5 days before and had an appointment with a doctor. When I came to re-register myself in the desk, the staff denied my reservation and instead asked me to register the regular one. Okay, I can accept this if he gave me an adequate explanation, which of course, he didn't. Instead, he said that the doctor I had an appointment to "is not working there again". Very ridiculous because I can see the nametag of the doctor in the hallway.

Fast forward, I've registered a regular one and waited for my turn. It took almost 5 hours to get me done there. Here's the recap:
09.30 - 10.00 : waiting for checkup by the nurse
10.00 - 13.00 : after waiting for 3 hours, finally consulted with the doctor
14.00 : USG finally taken

Clearly, the staffs are very disrespectful with the patients' time. At the doctor's room, I was also treated badly. The doctor who assigned to check on me suddenly leave without saying a word neither to me or his colleagues in his room (he could say to them to at least take care of me, i.e.). This didn't happen only to me. Another lady who already lying in the bed also had to wait for long because the doctor who was taking care of her suddenly leave.

These all are very disappointing for a big hospital like Sardjito. Please, educate the doctors better on treating the patients better. I was also saddened by the fact that in that waiting room, there were quite a few old women waiting in wheelchair for their turn FOR HOURS. Please, fix your work better and change/revamp the work distribution to be more effective.

All in all, I'll not go here again. I better go to another hospital who knows to appreciate my time.

TL;DR : don't go here if you are the kind of person who really appreciate your time.

Also, a moral value: if you are already here, starting to be upset with the system, wondering if other people had experienced the same as you do and thus scroling through GMaps review, don't be afraid to question the staff everything you need to know as clear as possible. Surely, it was my mistake too who was easily being tossed around here and there.

We have to complete the covid screening form and body temperature is also checked  before we come in to this hospital. If you are  in unwell conditions and you have several symptoms of covid, you have to check in Gabriel clinic to have special check. Gabriel clinic is at the old building near of emergency room that is separated from patient without covid symptoms. The outpatient services without covid symptoms is served in new building that's called Boromeus. This hospital always clean the public area with disinfectant for several times. So we didn't worry to medical check in this hospital.

No positive covid tested patient there, only for the negative tested. If you're local people or Indonesian citizenship, you can use BPJS(it's similar to insurance but only available for people with Indonesian citizenship) and there's a verification needed if you choose BPJS for the services/payment method to avoid fraudulent. The parking is large enough, it's underground. Anyway, it's a good hospital, and I can recommend this for you ONLY IF YOU'RE SICK. You don't want to stay in hospital if you're healthy, do you? 😂

Tugu Yogyakarta Station offers a comfortable, strategic, and culturally rich travel experience. Overall it delivers excellent facilities, well integrated transportation access, and a charming atmosphere for heritage lovers. A must visit gateway when arriving in Yogyakarta.

Busy, central station with good connections

Stasiun Tugu is one of the main train stations located right in the heart of Yogyakarta. Most intercity trains depart from or arrive here, including the train to the airport, so it's a really important hub for travelers.

It’s almost always busy, especially on weekends or during the holiday season, so be prepared for the crowd. One thing I find a bit confusing is figuring out which platform to go to when entering from the south gate, it's not always very clear (haha), so it might take a few minutes to get your bearings.

Overall, it's a convenient and well-connected station, especially if you're exploring Java by train!

Limited info here, except to reiterate what others have said. If you want a long-distance train and not the airport train, make sure you choose the right door!

Once through the electronic gates for the YIA airport trains, I was a bit surprised by how compact the waiting area was, and how little signage there was. Thankfully, the locals are friendly and helpful. Better than signage, I think.

I'd love to explore the entire Indonesian rail system, but I'm thinking I'd need to chill a good bit to make that work. Always good to know where you are and where you're going. :)

If you don’t know something, you can ask the station staff or security officers. They will help you kindly and politely. This is what I love about Yogyakarta—people here are so friendly. You don’t need to feel shy or scared; they will answer your questions with a smile.

Tugu Station is always bustling with activity. On a bright morning, cars and motorbikes are neatly parked, and the area around the station is clean. The fresh morning air adds a sense of renewal, symbolizing the hopes and spirit of Yogyakarta’s people. This station stands as a witness to their journeys, as they strive toward dreams and aspirations.

They provide free refills of clean drinking water in the waiting area. Additionally, this place is welcoming to tourists, as it offers a variety of traditional Yogyakarta cuisine upon arrival at the station. It’s perfect for those who don’t have time to buy local delicacies elsewhere. This station is also among the busiest railway stations in Indonesia.

Having recently visited the bustling Yogyakarta train station, I was thoroughly impressed by the efficiency and convenience it offered. From the moment I stepped onto the platform, I was greeted by helpful staff who guided me effortlessly through the ticketing process. Despite the station being quite crowded, the layout was well-organized, and I never felt overwhelmed. The facilities were clean and well-maintained. Overall, my experience at this train station was nothing short of excellent, and I would highly recommend it to anyone.

Lempuyangan Station is one of the main train stations in Yogyakarta, Indonesia, and is located in Bausasran, Danurejan.
Facilities include a large, Class B station with 11 train tracks. It serves mixed-class and economy intercity trains, agglomeration trains, and the Yogyakarta Commuter Line.
Self-service check-in for boarding passes is also available, along with 24-hour service, restrooms, a prayer room, a children's play area, food stalls, and more.

Several trains stop at the station, including the economy intercity trains: Bogowonto, Gajahwong, Sri Tanjung, Pasundan, Kahuripan, Bengawan, Jaka Tingkir, and Logawa; the Yogyakarta Commuter Line (Yogyakarta-Palur); and the Joglosemarkerto agglomeration train (Solo Balapan-Semarang Tawang).

The station is large and spacious, but without portable air conditioning, the inside can feel very hot and stuffy. Even with large fans installed, they don’t seem to reduce the heat effectively. The entrance and exit areas are often crowded with unofficial parking, which can make it difficult for passengers to get in and out. On the positive side, the facilities are fairly complete, including a ticket counter, souvenir shop, toilets, and a prayer room.

destination: Office of the Mayor of Yogyakarta
text:
This morning, I went to the City Hall, intending to take an ID card that had been corrected, but it turned out,  it wasn't done.  It seems that there is indeed an effort towards fast, precise service but it is not yet supported by adequate resources.
The Population and Civil Registry Service @Dinas DukCaPil servers often hang, take long to load and are unresponsive.
Hopefully there will be improvements soon so that the goal of the Yogyakarta City Government towards qualified services ❤  JSS Jogjakarta Smart Services will be realized soon 🍻 CHEERS 👏
Regarding the situation, the current situation at City Hall compared to when I was active 9 years ago, is very... very different.
The building is physically well maintained but also seems to be ^getting denser.^
Hopefully this density is due  ❤🌹  to the work of serving the community which is increasingly diverse in type and evidence that community members really take advantage of public service facilities at City Hall. 🙏🏻 🌈 🔝
Bravo to our Jogjakarta City Government Staff and keep on serving the spirit. 👏🙏🏻🌷

destination: Special Region of Yogyakarta Governor's Office

Kantor gubernur ini salah satu kantor yg ada bangunan bersejarah nya terutama bangsal Kepatihan. Kantor ini juga masih melestarikan bangunan bersejarah itu, dirawat dengan baik, dilakukan perbaikan perbaikan. Sehingga bangunan nya itu terlihat masih bagus. Pengalaman yg sangat excited bisa berproses disini, bisa mengetahui bagaimana program kerja di Kepatihan.. tempat nya pun luas, pelayanan di semua biro jugaa baik, fasilitas lengkap sehingga kita terasa nyaman ketika berkunjung kesanaa..

destination: Special Region of Yogyakarta Governor's Office
text:
Secara keseluruhan kompleks Kepatihan Danurejan ini merupakan bangunan berarsitektur tradisional Jawa yang terdiri atas pendapa, dalem ageng, gandhok, dan gadri. Di dalam kompleks juga terdapat fasilitas berupa masjid Sulthoni Kepatihan, selain itu juga dilengkapi dengan bangunan pelengkap seperti Bale Mangu dan lain-lain. Keseluruhan kompleks dibatasi pagar keliling dengan pintu masuk utama di sisi selatan.
🌾
Bangunan ini ditetapkan sebagai Cagar Budaya dengan Peraturan Menteri Kebudayaan dan Pariwisata No. PM.07/PW.007/MKP/2010. Bangunan Kompleks Kepatihan terletak di Jalan Malioboro No. 52-54, Suryatmajan, Danurejan, Yogyakarta.
⚜️

destination: Special Region of Yogyakarta Governor's Office
text:
The office is open at 08.00 - 16.00 on Mon - Thurs, and 08.00 - 14.00 on Fri. If you want to send letters, invitation, proposal, or any document addressed to the Governor or the Vice Governor, go directly to Biro Umum dan Protokol located on the north of the mosque. The Biro hosts the central archiving system for the Governor and Vice Governor. It is located on the ground floor, next to a photocopy services. After you put your letter there, you will get a sheet of paper containing relevant number so that you will be able to track your letter.

Jika akan mengirim surat, undangan, atau dokumen apapun kepada Gubernur atau Wakil Gubernur, silakan langsung menuju Biro Umum dan Protokol yang berada di sebelah utara masjid. Bagian Arsip berada di lantai dasar di sebelah fotokopian. Bagian inilah yang menerima semua dokumen yang dialamatkan kepada Gubernur dan Wakil Gubernur. Setelah memasukkan dokumen di sana, akan memperoleh slip yang berisi detail untuk mengecek progress pemrosesan dokumen tersebut.

Gedung yang luas dan megah. Area parkir luas. Ruangan bersih harum dan dingin. Bangunan empat lantai ini begitu nyaman. Berada di pusat kota berhadapan dengan Taman Makam Pahlawan Kusuma Negara (semaki). Sayang lift atau elevatornya kurang berfungsi dengan baik. Gedung ini sering digunakan untuk test penerima beasiswa LPDP (Lembava Pengelola Dana Pedidikan) dan test masuk STAN (Sekolah Tinggi Akuntansi Negara).
Mushollanya terletak di lantai pertama, fasilitas karpet sajadahnya lembut tebal dan halus, pesan di picasso carpet jogja (XT SQUARE UMBULHARJO ). cara pemasangannya rapih dan profesional

A free family educational park on the West side of Gajah Wong River in a middie class residential complex in Pandeyan, Umbulharjo District, Yogyakarta City. Small kids can play at the playgrounds. A cool green garden with lovely statues of wild animals, a dove house, gazebos and a traffic education arena. A clean mosque next to it. A clean river walk makes a cool area with a view post, green plants and tables. Motorbikes and cars can park on the road side. Clean restrooms with squat toilets.

Hidden park in town! The playground is actually quite nice with all the slides and all those kids' stuff. However the maintenance is not so good. Some parts of the main slide seem to be vulnerable, and because they dont put pavements below so there is quite much dirty sand on the slides.
There is no cycling path for adults, yet pushbikes for children can still fit because the place is not crowded.
There are also many mosquitos, better be careful!

Gajah Wong Educational Park jadi salah satu ruang terbuka hijau yang nyaman di tengah kota Yogyakarta. Sebuah taman terbuka hijau yang bisa diakses oleh semua orang secara cuma-cuma. Menyuguhkan pemandangan hijau terhampar di tengah padatnya pemukiman Kota Yogyakarta. Untuk menuju kesini harus melewati Perumahan Gambiran. Banyak jalan menuju kesini, hampir semua gang akan berujung kesini. Akses jalan perumahan berupa paving block, kendaraan R2 dan R4 bisa diparkir di tepi jalan sepanjang taman ini tanpa ada pungutan parkir. Menurut tata tertib yang terpampang, jam operasional taman bermain anak adalah jam 07.00-17.00 dan dilarang berdagang disini, jadi harap membawa bekal makanan dan minuman sendiri. Tersedia beberapa gazebo untuk sekedar beristirahat. Di taman ini tersedia fasilitas pendukung seperti gazebo, Playground, spot untuk berswapoto, kata air, kolam ikan dengan patung gajah dan orang (sebagai pengingat legenda nama gajah wong), kandang burung merpati, toilet, masjid yang berada di atas taman. Taman ini lebih cocok sebagai taman bermain anak, anak bisa bebas berlarian dan mencoba aneka permainan. Nampaknya perlu ditingkatkan lagi perawatan taman ini seperti kebersihan kandang merpati, kolam tanpa ikan, kotoran kelelawar di gardu pandang. Tapi secara keseluruhan taman ini telah membantu meningkatkan kesehatan jasmani dan rohani serta membahagiakan anak. Sangat nyaman untuk dikunjungi sebagai alternatif tempat mengasuh anak.

During my time living in Yogyakarta, this has been one of the open spaces in the city I visit most often. Whether you’re looking for a spot to jog, take a walk, have a casual catch-up with friends, or even enjoy a small picnic, Wisdom Park is a great fit for all kinds of visitors. The area is shaded, clean, and generally calm on weekdays. Even on weekends, when more people come by, it still feels pleasant and comfortable. With so many open spaces available in Yogyakarta, this one stands out as a highly recommended alternative, especially if you’re around UGM and UNY area and want something close by.

Wisdom Park UGM is a spot on campus that’s perfect for some outdoor relaxation or exercise. The jogging track around the park is a great place for a morning run or walk, and there are plenty of park benches where you can sit and enjoy the surroundings. The area is quite shaded and green, creating a serene and refreshing atmosphere.

The park also has a lake, which adds to the tranquil atmosphere. However, the last time I visited, the lake was somewhat dirty, which detracted from the overall experience. With a bit more care, this area could become a real highlight of the park.

However, there are some areas that could use a bit more attention. The plants and flowers around the park seem to lack proper maintenance, and with some care, they could make the park even more vibrant. There’s also a lot of space in the park that could be utilized better, perhaps by adding some gym equipment for outdoor workouts.

The facilities, like the toilets, are decent and well-maintained, which is always a plus.

Wisdom Park at Gadjah Mada University (UGM) is a thoughtfully designed open space located within the campus area in Sleman, Yogyakarta. Surrounded by trees and landscaped gardens, the park offers a refreshing contrast to the academic buildings that dominate much of the university grounds. Its layout features wide walking paths, a tranquil lake, benches, and several shaded spots, making it a comfortable place for relaxation, study, or light conversation. Despite being relatively simple in structure, the space is carefully maintained and visually appealing, especially during the early morning or late afternoon when natural lighting enhances its calm atmosphere.

As a student of Universitas Gadjah Mada, Wisdom Park often feels like a quiet retreat from the academic pressures of campus life. Many students come here to read, reflect, or simply rest in between classes. The presence of water and green space helps create a peaceful environment that encourages mindfulness and serenity. While not necessarily a major tourist attraction, it serves its purpose well as a community green space, supporting the university’s educational philosophy of balance between knowledge, nature, and well-being.

However, its popularity among students and visitors can sometimes lead to crowding during weekends or special events, slightly diminishing the quietness it normally offers. Facilities such as restrooms or designated reading corners are somewhat limited, so visitors looking for extended stays may need to plan accordingly. Nonetheless, Wisdom Park remains a pleasant and valuable addition to the UGM campus, fostering a deeper appreciation for nature within an academic setting.

For Gadjah Mada's alumnus like me, it was once a place called Lembah UGM, now it became a park for exercise and communal place. Perfect for morning/afternoon walks or jogging, especially at the weekend. It was clean, lots of trees and plants, lots of cats too. 😻 …

Starting my mornings with a jog at Wisdom Park is an absolute delight. This place, once just a simple valley, has transformed into something truly remarkable. Strolling along the jogging track, taking in the serene lake and surrounding trees, is incredibly refreshing. Kudos to the creative minds behind revamping this spot. This place is offering a diverse range of activities and facilities, such as: A well-kept jogging track, A picturesque lake, children's playground, futness area, food court, etc.
With its meticulous maintenance, the park offers an oasis of tranquility away from city chaos. Whether you seek relaxation or an active workout, Wisdom Park has you covered. Amid lush greenery, it's the perfect place to unwind. Open to all and free to enter, Wisdom Park UGM is a true gem in Yogyakarta.

Wisdom Park is a comfortable place for exercising. However, the paving is somewhat uneven, which might be uncomfortable for some runners, but if you're skilled enough, it might not be an issue. The atmosphere is quite peaceful, and the foliage is regularly swept by staff every day.

It's somewhat busy on Saturdays and Sundays.

Great cozy park with various sports facilities. There are tracks that are usually used for walking and jogging, as well as several other sports facilities. Suitable for exercising in the morning or evening with fresh air near campus. You can use a lotion like 'Autan' during the rainy season, because in some areas, there are mosquitoes roaming around.

Its more than just the lake. Though it might confusing for you to understand tha layout from the pictures here, its a great place for you to get some sun in the morning while in Jogja. It’s jogging friendly, but if you want to just take a walk, its okay too. Enjoy 😉 …

Very nice to have such cozy park in the city. This place is very well managed and maintained, tidy, and clean. Cleaning services are working hard to keep this place clean and comfortable for visitors.
Wisdom Park is usually used for jogging, sports, relaxation, even for a cup of coffee.
Never imagine decades ago, this place will be transformed into such a well maintained park like nowadays.
Great place, will be my first choice for jogging and enjoying holiday with family.
nice green space inside UGM campus and great for jogging, chill walks, or just sitting by the lake. it’s kinda quiet, clean, and free to enter. perfect spot to relax!!

What a gem of a place in Yogyakarta! which is surprisingly lacking in this type of green, urban parks. Situated on the UGM campus, there is forest canopy walk, tree-lined artificial lake, paved walkways along the river banks, abundant flower blossoms littering  the grounds. Just the river needs a bit of a cleanup...happy to volunteer!

Wisdom Park is a small park, but provides a natural elongated walking trail starting from Wisma MMUGM to the lake. Please note at the end of January 2024 the Northern side (the lake) is being renovated. The complete circuit is about 2 km long. Mature trees provide a cool cover, but some parts of the paving blocks trail need some repairs. A small gate is open across from the campus Mosque, or you can enter from the main entrance once you enter the UGM main campus. There is a BNI'46 Food Court that opens about 7AM. The cafe offers specialty coffee from various regions of Indonesia.

Cool park in the middle of city, there is running track too. It's split into 2 section, the North side which is the lake part and the river part on the South. Both pretty chill place to spend your time.

Good place for workout and jogs.

Wisdom Park is an open space that has a jogging track, basketball court, volleyball court, amphitheater, picnic area and also several spots for meditation which are located within one area of ​​the Gadjah Mada University campus.  Access to Wisdom Park can be via Jalan Agro (weekdays) or from the Grha Sabha entrance (weekends).  At Wisdom Park there are also several culinary stalls, which you can visit for breakfast.  The place is beautiful, entry is free, there are quite complete amenities although the bathroom is a bit dirty, every morning the area is swept clean.  And there is a fairly large and free parking area.

Pertama kalinya jalan-jalan di lembah UGM. Kondisi baru aja hujan jadi jalanan sekitar lembah agak becek dan banyak nyamuk. Untuk olahraga lari kurang rekomendasi karena jalanan yg tidak rata dan banyak jalanan yg berlumut sehingga bisa menyebabkan kepleset. Untuk sekedar menikmati keindahan lembah sudah cukup.
Jalanan juga cukup sempit, mungkin bisa dilebarkan untuk menjaga keamanan dan kenyamanan bersama. Apabila ada yg mau mendahului juga agak sempit.
Kondisi waduknya cukup bersih namun sayang tidak banyak ikannya.
Spot untuk duduk juga sedikit dan aksesnya becek karena genangan air.

So far masih oke dan bagus.

Tempat yg bisa dimanfaatkan utk banyak kegiatan. Mau olahraga ada jogging track dan beberapa sarana olahraga, mau kumpul dengan teman-teman juga banyak kursi atau pendopo. Bahkan ada yang mancing di waduknya. Suasana menenangkan.

Kelebihan
1. Lokasi strategis di dalam kampus Universitas Gadjah Mada.
2. Suasana alam yang indah dan tenang.
3. Fasilitas rekreasi seperti danau, taman, dan jogging track.
4. Cocok untuk relaksasi, piknik, dan kegiatan outdoor.
5. Akses mudah ke perpustakaan dan fasilitas kampus lain.

Kekurangan
1. Jogging Tracking perlu perbaikan di benerapa tempat, karena jadi berlumpur setelah hujan.
2. Beberapa fasilitas memerlukan perawatan.
3. Kurangnya pencahayaan pada malam hari.

Saran
1. Datang pagi atau sore untuk menghindari panas.
2. Bawa bekal dan minuman.
3. Jangan membuang sampah sembarangan.
4. Menggunakan sepeda atau berjalan kaki untuk menikmati suasana.

Ulasan
Lembah UGM adalah destinasi wisata alam yang menyenangkan dan edukatif. Cocok untuk mahasiswa, keluarga, dan wisatawan yang mencari ketenangan. Perlu perbaikan fasilitas dan kebersihan untuk meningkatkan kenyamanan pengunjung.

Taman cocok untuk bermain sama anak2 atau buat rekreasi.
Di sana tersedia juga beberapa kran air minum gratis, areanya juga luas tidak terlalu jauh dari masjid UGM..
area olahraga sekaligus bisa buat rekreasi anak, ada taman dan danau juga. Bisa untuk latihan dayungan. Tersedia fasilitas refill air minum, jogging track, sky bridge

Selama bulan Ramadhan di sepanjang jalan lembah UGM akan berjajar pedagang yang menjajakan takjil buat buka puasa. Cukup lengkap dan bervariasi. Mulai dari makanan hingga minuman. Dari yang tradisional hingga modern. Snack ringan dan berat ready. Keramaian mulai nampak jam empat hingga sesudah berbuka puasa.

UGM Valley is a great place for jogging, picnicking, or just hanging out with friends. It's very shady, with large trees scattered on both sides, and a gentle breeze. There's also a cafe at the entrance, I forget what it's called, so if you're feeling lazy on a park bench, you can just hang out there.

Entrance is free, and there's no parking fee either. Lastly, if you hang out between 5 and 6 p.m., you'll get bitten by a lot of mosquitoes, haha...
